description_long: This object is a vintage personal computer setup, featuring a beige CRT monitor with a thick, rectangular bezel and a slightly convex glass screen. The monitor sits atop a matching computer base unit with two horizontal floppy disk drives and a subtle power button. A full-sized keyboard with raised keys and a coiled cable is positioned in front, accompanied by a single-button mouse connected via a straight cord. The monitor's rear is boxy with horizontal ventilation slits, and the entire assembly is constructed from a matte, hard plastic material. The design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a uniform beige color throughout.
description: A vintage beige CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, paired with a matching base, keyboard, and mouse, all in a utilitarian, matte plastic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT monitor's screen, thick bezel, dual floppy drives, keyboard, and mouse.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's deep, boxy rear, ventilation slits, and the profile of the keyboard and mouse.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the monitor with ventilation slits and cable connections, as well as the back of the base unit.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the monitor's rear profile, the keyboard, and the mouse from the opposite side.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, rectangular, and made of matte beige plastic with slightly rounded corners and no visible decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, integrated pedestal base that is rectangular and solid, matching the beige plastic of the monitor.
attribute_button_placement: A single power button is visible on the lower right front of the base unit, below the monitor screen.
